lab_description: All US federal research laboratories across agencies: DOE (17 labs), NIH (27 institutes), NASA (10 centers), NOAA (7 labs), NIST (6 labs), USDA ARS (90+ labs), DoD (ARL/NRL/AFRL), EPA (ORD), USGS (7 centers), and more. Collectively the largest scientific research enterprise in the world.

categories.name: Fundamental Science (DOE Office of Science)
categories.description: Basic energy sciences, high energy physics, nuclear physics, biological/environmental research, advanced scientific computing, fusion. DOE SC funds 6 programs across 10 labs.

source_mappings.branch: Physics group + Physical Sciences group + Life/Environmental Sciences group
Physical Sciences domain + Life Sciences domain (basic research subsets)

cross_domain_refs.concept: basic research underpins all applied domains

categories.agency_labs: DOE: Argonne, Brookhaven, Fermilab, LBNL, Oak Ridge, SLAC, Ames, Princeton Plasma, Thomas Jefferson, PNNL

categories.name: Biomedical & Clinical Research (NIH)
categories.description: NIH intramural research plus 27 institutes/centers. Genomics, disease mechanisms, drug development, clinical trials, public health.

source_mappings.branch: Health Sciences > Medicine (52.7M works) + Neuroscience + Pharmacology
Full taxonomy (NCBI is part of NIH NLM)

categories.agency_labs: NIH: NCI, NIAID, NHLBI, NINDS, NIEHS, NHGRI, NLM/NCBI + 20 more institutes

categories.name: Earth & Space Science (NASA Centers)
categories.description: Earth observation, planetary exploration, heliophysics, astrophysics, aeronautics. 10 NASA centers.

source_mappings.branch: ALL (4,849 keywords — GCMD is NASA's taxonomy)
Earth and Planetary Sciences + Aerospace Engineering + Space and Planetary Science

categories.agency_labs: NASA: GSFC, JPL, Ames, Langley, Glenn, Marshall, Stennis, Johnson, Kennedy, Armstrong

categories.name: Ocean & Atmospheric Research (NOAA)
categories.description: Weather prediction, ocean monitoring, fisheries science, coastal management, climate services. NOAA's 7 research labs.

source_mappings.branch: EARTH SCIENCE > ATMOSPHERE + OCEANS + CLIMATE INDICATORS

categories.agency_labs: NOAA: GFDL, PMEL, AOML, NSSL, ESRL/GSL, GLERL, NCEI

categories.name: Standards & Measurement Science (NIST)
categories.description: Physical measurement standards, materials characterization, cybersecurity standards, quantum standards, manufacturing metrology.
categories.agency_labs: NIST: 6 research labs in Gaithersburg + Boulder

categories.name: Agricultural Research (USDA ARS/FS)
categories.description: Crop science, animal science, soil science, food safety, forestry, pest management. USDA's Agricultural Research Service (90+ labs) and Forest Service labs.
categories.agency_labs: USDA: ARS (90+ labs), Forest Service Research Stations (5), ERS, NASS

categories.name: Defense Research (DoD Labs)
categories.description: Military technology, materials under extreme conditions, communications, cybersecurity, autonomous systems. Army/Navy/Air Force/DARPA labs.

source_mappings.branch: Military Technology, Weaponry, and National Defense

categories.agency_labs: DoD: ARL, NRL, AFRL, DARPA, Lincoln Lab (MIT), Sandia (shared w/ DOE)

categories.name: Environmental Protection Research (EPA)
categories.description: Air/water quality, toxicology, risk assessment, pollution control, ecological health. EPA's research enterprise.

source_mappings.branch: Environmental Science > Pollution + Health/Toxicology + Environmental Engineering

categories.agency_labs: EPA: ORD (3 national labs, 4 centers), regional labs

categories.name: Earth Science & Natural Resources (USGS)
categories.description: Earthquake monitoring, volcanic hazards, water resources, mineral resources, ecosystems, geologic mapping.

source_mappings.branch: EARTH SCIENCE > SOLID EARTH + LAND SURFACE + TERRESTRIAL HYDROSPHERE
Earth and Planetary Sciences > Geology + Geophysics + Earth-Surface Processes

categories.name: Climate & Earth System Science (Multi-Agency)
categories.description: Climate modeling, observations, impacts, mitigation. USGCRP spans NASA, NOAA, DOE, NSF, USDA, EPA, USGS, DOI.

source_mappings.branch: CLIMATE INDICATORS + ATMOSPHERE + OCEANS + CRYOSPHERE + PALEOCLIMATE
Global and Planetary Change + Atmospheric Science + Ecological Modeling

cross_domain_refs.concept: climate models on HPC, observation systems

categories.agency_labs: Multi-agency: USGCRP coordinates across 13 federal agencies

categories.name: Cybersecurity & Critical Infrastructure (Multi-Agency)
categories.description: Grid security, network defense, cryptography, infrastructure protection. NIST, DOE, DoD, DHS labs.

source_mappings.branch: Computer Science > Computer Networks + Information Systems

categories.agency_labs: Multi-agency: NIST, DOE labs (INL, Sandia, PNNL), DoD (NSA, CYBERCOM), DHS (CISA)

categories.name: Advanced Manufacturing (Multi-Agency)
categories.description: Additive manufacturing, robotics, smart manufacturing, digital twins. DOE, NIST, DoD Manufacturing USA institutes.

source_mappings.branch: Industrial and Manufacturing Engineering + Mechanical Engineering

categories.agency_labs: Multi-agency: DOE (ORNL MDF), NIST (MML), DoD Manufacturing USA, NSF (ERC)

cross_domain_bridges.all_labs_aspect: synchrotrons, neutron sources, sequencing centers, computing centers, telescopes, research vessels
cross_domain_bridges.note: Shared infrastructure that serves researchers from all domains

cross_domain_bridges.all_labs_aspect: satellite constellation, ground stations, data archives
cross_domain_bridges.note: Multi-agency observation systems feeding identical datasets to different missions
DOE JGI and NIH NHGRI both sequence genomes but for different purposes (energy vs health)
USGCRP coordinates 13 agencies — the most multi-agency topic
DOE synchrotrons serve materials researchers from all agencies
Every agency is integrating AI — cross-cutting like computing itself

inter_agency_tensions: DOE and NIH both fund genomics (JGI vs NHGRI) — different organisms, different goals, overlapping methods
NASA, NOAA, and USGS all do earth observation — different instruments, overlapping geophysical variables
DOE and DoD both do materials science — different performance requirements, shared characterization tools
EPA and USGS both monitor water quality — regulatory vs scientific framing of same data
Climate science: NASA does global modeling, NOAA does weather/seasonal prediction, DOE does Earth system modeling — same physics, different timescales
methodology_notes: Analyzed 74K keywords through the lens of the entire US federal laboratory system. Each agency's labs see the same scientific landscape through different mission lenses. Key insight: most major scientific domains are studied by 3+ agencies simultaneously, creating natural taxonomy alignment opportunities but also terminology drift. DOE OSTI categories align best with DOE lab structure. NASA GCMD aligns perfectly with NASA centers. OpenAlex captures the academic research that all labs participate in. NCBI is directly an NIH product. UNESCO and LoC provide the broadest cross-agency vocabulary.
